utility_evaluation.REASONING:
Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 corresponding sections. Now check if each is fully specified per the format: 
- health_coverage: Requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan provides provider, physical_cost, mental_cost, total_cost, benefits. Fully specified.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides provider, base_cost, add_on with cost, total_cost, benefits. Fully specified.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. Plan provides provider, base_cost, add_on with cost, total_cost, benefits. Fully specified.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan states N/A and notes membership declined; while no costs listed, it explicitly addresses the item. Considered specified as none.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan provides monthly and annual totals. Fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three providers: PrimeCare Premium Plan for health (physical and mental), PrimeHome Premium Coverage for property with Technology Protection add-on, and PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel with Adventure Travel Coverage add-on. I matched each named item to the ground-truth ratings. PrimeCare Premium Plan has ratings for both physical and mental health in the dataset. PrimeHome Premium Coverage and Technology Protection are listed under property_protection and property_enhancement_services respectively. PrimeTravel Premium Plan and Adventure Travel Coverage are listed under mobility_coverage and travel_enhancement_services respectively.
